bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about the concept of “Critical Regionalism,” popularized by architecture critic Kenneth Frampton, for 10 points each.
parts: The concept originated in the essay “The Grid and the Pathway,” regarding the architecture of this country. An architect and composer from this country designed the exterior of the Philips Pavilion for Le Corbusier.

To introduce the concept, Frampton cites this architect’s Bagsvaerd Church, whose interior shell is inspired by a Chinese pagoda roof. This architect is better known for designing the Sydney Opera House.

Frampton singles out the work of Tadao Ando, who designed this building attached to the Alpha Resort Hotel on Hokkaido. This religious building consists of a glass-and-steel cube intersecting with a concrete prism.
answers: the Church on the Water [or the Chapel on the Water; do not accept or prompt on “Water Temple”]
answers_sanitized: the Church on the Water [or the Chapel on the Water; do not accept or prompt on "Water Temple"]
